description: Two agents repeatedly play Prisoner's Dilemma. Each round, each agent chooses cooperate (C) or defect (D). Payoffs are CC=(3,3), CD=(0,5), DC=(5,0), DD=(1,1).
Two agents repeatedly play Prisoner's Dilemma with the standard payoff matrix CC=(3,3), CD=(0,5), DC=(5,0), DD=(1,1).
A longer repeated Prisoner's Dilemma game. The longer horizon tests whether agents maintain stable cooperative or retaliatory strategies.
Four agents repeatedly play a binary public-goods game. Each round, each agent either contributes the full endowment or keeps it. Contributions are multiplied by 1.6 and evenly redistributed.
Four agents repeatedly play a public-goods game with binary contribution actions.
Five agents play a lower-multiplier public-goods game. The lower multiplier increases the tension between individual incentives and social welfare.
